summary: Frontend-only verification of the new Vinyl Siding sub-category drop-down nesting (groupLinesBySubCategory + SectionAccordion). Logged in as hhunt6677@yahoo.com, opened an existing estimate in the Siding Quotes workspace on the Vinyl tab, and validated all 9 acceptance criteria via Playwright DOM assertions and screenshots. All sub-category UI behaviors work as specified.

integration_issues.flow: Autosave / qty persistence (likely PRE-EXISTING, not caused by sub-cat change)
integration_issues.issue: After filling qty=10 on a Conquest line via Playwright fill() + Tab, waiting 8s, then page.reload() — the qty input came back empty and section/VINYL totals were $0.00. React state DID update in-memory (sub-cat subtotal correctly showed $2,271.50 before reload), but the value did not survive a reload. This may simply be that autosave is debounced longer than 8s, or that this test estimate's qty was previously empty and the change wasn't flushed before reload. NOT related to the sub-category grouping change (which is purely client-side).

action_items: OPTIONAL: Confirm autosave debounce window for line qty edits on the estimate editor — qty=10 did not persist after an 8s wait + reload in this test. Main agent should verify whether this is expected debounce timing or a regression. Sub-category UI itself is unaffected.
critical_code_review_comments: SectionAccordion.jsx:139 — useState(subGroups ? new Set(subGroups.slice(0,1)...) : new Set()) is computed from subGroups which depends on `lines`. If the catalog ever returned no Conquest items at first paint and added them later, the initial-open set would be stale (React useState init only runs once). Low risk today since the catalog is static, but consider keying off section.title only.

subCategories.js — match functions use startsWith on the raw item name. If item names get translated (tItem(l.name, lang)) the user-visible label flows through tItem but the matching here still uses l.name (raw English), which is correct for grouping. Good. Just worth a comment so future devs don't accidentally swap to translated names.
seed_data_creation: none — used existing draft estimate EST-405137 (id 2fc4e383-1ef9-423d-b756-df4fc4dfdb47)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sub-category nesting under Vinyl Siding works as designed. data-testids used: section-Vinyl Siding, subcat-{conquest|coventry|odyssey|charter|vbb|shakes}, subcat-toggle-{...}. Conquest opens by default; click subcat-toggle-{id} to expand others. Each header has 2 div.font-mono-num children — first span has line count, the trailing div has the $ subtotal (use 'div.font-mono-num' selector specifically). Other sections (Siding Accessories, Vinyl Soffit with Siding, Tear-Off / Clean Up) render flat — confirmed 0 subcat elements inside.

verified_acceptance_criteria.conquest_default_expanded_others_collaps: PASS — 4 Conquest rows visible initially; Coventry rows = 0 before click
verified_acceptance_criteria.coventry_expands_to_8_lines: PASS — exactly 8 row-Vinyl Siding-Coventry* elements after click
verified_acceptance_criteria.charter_oak_expands_to_4_lines: PASS — exactly 4 row-Vinyl Siding-Charter Oak* elements after click
verified_acceptance_criteria.subcat_header_shows_label_count_subtotal: PASS — label, mono-num count, and $ subtotal all rendered
verified_acceptance_criteria.qty_updates_propagate_to_section_and_sub: PASS — qty=10 on Conquest Standard color Clap 4.5" .040 → section subtotal $2,271.50, Conquest subcat subtotal $2,271.50
verified_acceptance_criteria.lightbulb_pill_on_subcat_header_for_comm: PASS — Charter Oak .046 header shows yellow Lightbulb pill with count 1 (Charter Oak Standard color Dutch Lap 4.5" .046); Conquest has 0
verified_acceptance_criteria.other_sections_render_flat: PASS — Siding Accessories, Vinyl Soffit with Siding, Tear-Off / Clean Up have 0 subcat-* descendants
verified_acceptance_criteria.no_console_errors: PASS — 0 console errors during full flow
verified_acceptance_criteria.save_and_reload_preserves_structure: PASS for structure — all 6 subcats re-render after reload; qty persistence likely a separate autosave timing concern, see integration_issues
